items.headline: Avalanche, Wild trade punches in high-scoring Game 1
items.description:
DENVER -- For the first 11 minutes of Sunday’s Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round at Ball Arena, it was quiet.
Clearly the quiet was misleading because a few seconds later, it started. Goals. So, so many goals.

items.headline: Minnesota Wild Colorado Avalanche game 1 recap May 3 2026
items.description: DENVER -- Cale Makar scored twice in the third period and had an assist for the Colorado Avalanche, who recovered for a 9-6 win against the Minnesota Wild in Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round at Ball Arena on Sunday.

items.headline: Jakub Dobes comes through for Canadiens in Game 7
items.description:
TAMPA -- Jakub Dobes was justifiably named the first star of the Montreal Canadiens’ 2-1 win in Game 7 of their Eastern Conference First Round against the Tampa Bay Lightning on Sunday.
His reward?
A surprise shower of shaving cream, courtesy of fellow Canadiens goalies Jacob Fowler and Sam Montembeault,

items.headline: Canadiens find way to endure in Game 7 against Lightning
items.description: TAMPA -- Martin St. Louis entered the jubilant Montreal Canadiens dressing room and thrusted his arm in the air, igniting whoops from his euphoric players.

items.headline: Color of Hockey Renee Ng raising game for Wilkes University and Hong Kong
items.description: William Douglas has been writing The Color of Hockey blog since 2012. Douglas joined NHL.com in 2019 and writes about people of color in the sport. Today, as part of NHL.com's celebration of Asian & Pacific Islander Heritage month, he profiles Renee Ng, a forward with Hong Kong Women’s National

items.headline: Montreal Canadiens Tampa Bay Lightning Game 7 recap May 3 2026
items.description: TAMPA -- Alex Newhook scored the go-ahead goal at 11:07 of the third period, and the Montreal Canadiens eliminated the Tampa Bay Lightning with a 2-1 win in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Benchmark International Arena on Sunday.​

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.

items.headline: Newhook Canadiens highlight goal Lightning
items.description:
The only thing better than scoring a series-winning goal in a Game 7 is scoring a series-winning goal in a Game 7 with style.
Montreal Canadiens forward Alex Newhook did just that.
Newhook's third period goal against the Tampa Bay Lightning in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference First Round

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres Montreal Canadiens 2026 playoff lookahead
items.description:
The Buffalo Sabres will play the Montreal Canadiens in the Eastern Conference Second Round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
The Sabres won their first playoff series since 2006-07 with a 4-1 victory against the Boston Bruins in Game 6 of the first round at TD Garden in Boston on Friday.

items.headline: Minnesota Wild Jonas Brodin Joel Eriksson Ek injury status update Game 1
items.description:
DENVER -- Jonas Brodin and Joel Eriksson Ek did not play for the Minnesota Wild in Game 1 of the Western Conference Second Round against the Colorado Avalanche at Ball Arena on Sunday.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs first round schedule television results
items.description: Here are the complete results from the first round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs.

items.headline: Blackstone Valley Co-op hockey team gets visit from Patrice Bergeron Stanley Cup
items.description:
PAWTUCKET, Rhode Island -- The smiles were priceless.
The gym at Saint Raphael Academy was filled with players from the Blackstone Valley Co-op high school hockey team and their families. A surprise was in the offing, but nobody really knew what.
Then, suddenly, retired Boston Bruins star Patrice Bergeron walked

items.headline: Noah Dobson game-time decision for Montreal Canadiens in Game 7
items.description:
TAMPA -- Noah Dobson returned for the Montreal Canadiens in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference First Round against the Tampa Bay Lightning at Benchmark International Arena on Sunday.
The defenseman hadn’t played since sustaining an upper-body injury while blocking a shot against the Columbus Blue Jackets on April 11.

items.headline: William Karlsson could return to Vegas Golden Knights lineup in second round
items.description:
LAS VEGAS -- The Vegas Golden Knights should be well prepared to play the Anaheim Ducks in the Western Conference Second Round after defeating the Utah Mammoth in the first round.
They should be even stronger if William Karlsson returns from a lower-body injury too.

items.headline: Denver Barkey may start at center for Philadelphia in Game 2
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. -- Among the changes the Philadelphia Flyers could be considering for Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round against the Carolina Hurricanes at Lenovo Center on Monday (7 p.m. ET; ESPN, SN, TVAS, CBC) is starting Denver Barkey at center.
